name: Documentation
prompts: Download the full PDF of Tencent's 2025 interim report (complete financial report). Extract the financial data from the PDF, build an Excel financial model (including formulas and formatting), then create a presentation using an Ocean Gradient color scheme, and finally generate a Word report containing the full analysis (with a table of contents and headers/footers).

Convert my uploaded Markdown file into a beautifully formatted PDF document. Requirements: custom cover page (title + author + date), auto-generated table of contents, syntax-highlighted code blocks, tables with alternating row colors, and page numbers in the footer.

Extract key information from these 3 competitive analysis PDFs, compile an Excel competitive comparison matrix (feature dimensions x competitors), and generate a Word competitive analysis report including: market positioning comparison, feature gap analysis, strengths/weaknesses summary, and recommended action items.

Based on my uploaded weekly project report Excel, auto-generate a Word-format project status report including: project overview dashboard (progress percentage, risk level, milestone status), this week's completed items, next week's plan, risks and blockers, resource needs. Use a professional project report template layout.

name: Financial Services
prompts: Give me a deep analysis of Tencent Holdings (00700.HK) — should I buy it? Cover technical analysis, fundamentals, market sentiment, and risk, and give me a clear BUY/SELL/HOLD recommendation with a specific action plan.
Which is a better investment — BYD or CATL? Run a systematic bull vs bear debate analysis and give me a final position sizing recommendation.
Build a comparable company analysis for Snowflake (SNOW), focusing on cloud data platform peers, including EV/Revenue, EV/GP, Rule of 40 and other key metrics.

Help me value NVIDIA with a full DCF model, considering Bear/Base/Bull scenarios for AI chip demand. Base case assumes 25% revenue CAGR over 5 years. Output an Excel with WACC sensitivity and terminal value sensitivity analysis.

Analyze Meta's (META) latest quarterly earnings, focusing on: ad revenue vs street expectations, Reality Labs loss trends, AI investment ROI (Capex guidance), user growth and engagement data. Output an 8-10 page Earnings Update Report with charts.

Help me build a comprehensive financial plan: age 45, married, 2 children (ages 10 and 14), household income $800K, liquid assets $5M. Goals: early retirement at 55, college education funds for both children, estate planning. Risk tolerance: moderate-conservative. Cover retirement planning, education planning, tax optimization, and estate arrangements.

name: Data Analysis & Visualization
prompts: Please conduct a complete “E-commerce business health + customer value” analysis based on UCI’s Online Retail.xlsx dataset (line-item order transactions).Objectives:1. Build a data dictionary* Explain each field’s meaning* Identify the data granularity (what one row represents)* Propose potential primary keys / unique identifiers* Identify time fields, monetary fields, and country fields* Include your business-meaning hypotheses, and clearly label any uncertainties2. Data quality audit* Missingness and outliers (e.g., extreme values in Quantity and UnitPrice, negative values, cancelled invoices where InvoiceNo starts with “C”, etc.)* Duplicate records and abnormal country/time distributions* Produce a “cleaning rules checklist” where each rule includes: rationale, impact scope, and a rollback strategy3. Core KPI dashboard (monthly)* GMV (excluding cancelled orders), number of orders, AOV, number of active purchasing users, repeat purchase rate, refund/cancellation rate* Use Plotly to build interactive charts (trends, distributions, Top countries/products)4. Segmentation and LTV* RFM segmentation (including justification for parameter choices)* A simplified cohort-based LTV: first-purchase month → subsequent-month contributions5. Anomaly detection* Detect unusual fluctuations in GMV and/or order volume* For each anomaly, provide: the specific dates, plausible root-cause hypotheses, and how to validate them with additional data or checks6. Conclusions and actions* Provide 5 executable recommendations. For each recommendation include: expected impact metrics, an experiment/validation design, and key risks or caveatsDeliverables:* A structured report (Executive Summary + key charts + conclusions)Data download link:[https://archive.ics.uci.edu/static/public/352/online+retail.zip](https://archive.ics.uci.edu/static/public/352/online+retail.zip)

Produce a complete analysis report on “macro labor market conditions + regional divergence” based on the USDA ERS county-level Unemployment & Median Household Income Excel dataset (2000–2023).Please follow these steps in your output:Data explorationData dictionary (field definitions)Missingness rate (overall and by key fields)Time span coverageAvailability and consistency checks for county/state identifiers (e.g., FIPS / state / county fields)Metric constructionUnemployment rate: YoY, MoM, and 3-year moving averageMedian household income: level vs YoY growth (if CPI is not provided, do a nominal analysis and explicitly state the limitation)Regional divergence: within-state percentile gap, and national Gini / Theil index (compute if feasible)Identify “anomalous counties”Top N counties with the largest unemployment shock from 2020 to 2021Top N counties with the slowest recovery from 2022 to 2023 (define your own recovery metric and explain it clearly)Visualization (Plotly)National trend with quantile bandsState-level / county-level heatmaps (if geographic boundaries are unavailable, use Top-N tables + bar charts instead)Policy / business implicationsProvide 5 actionable conclusions with clear reasoning grounded in the dataDownload link:https://www.ers.usda.gov/media/5494/unemployment-and-median-household-income-for-the-united-states-states-and-counties-2000-23.xlsx?v=67259

Using World Bank World Development Indicators data (fetchable online), conduct a 'Global GDP per Capita vs. Human Development Index (HDI) Correlation Analysis' covering 2000–2023.Requirements:1) Data acquisition and cleaning: Retrieve GDP per capita (PPP) and HDI data from the World Bank API or UNDP data sources; handle missing countries/years and report data coverage rates2) Interactive scatter plot: X-axis GDP per capita (log scale), Y-axis HDI, colored by continent (Asia/Europe/Africa/Americas/Oceania), bubble size representing population; hover to show country name and exact values3) Time slider animation: Draggable slider to view the global distribution for each year from 2000 to 20234) Trend line analysis: HDI trend comparison for the Top 10 fastest-improving countries vs. the Bottom 10 stagnating/declining countries5) Correlation quantification: Calculate GDP–HDI correlation coefficients by income group (low/lower-middle/upper-middle/high income); analyze the 'diminishing HDI returns at higher income' phenomenon6) Output 3 policy recommendations based on data findings, tailored to countries at different development stages

Using United Nations Population Division data (fetchable online), conduct a 'Global Population Structure Transition' visualization analysis covering 1950–2100 (including projections).Requirements:1) Data acquisition: Retrieve age-structure, fertility rate, and urbanization data from the UN Population Division; document data sources and projection methodology2) Population pyramid animation: One frame per decade (1950/1960/.../2050/2100), with China/India/USA/Japan displayed side by side, showing the structural shift from 'pyramid' to 'inverted pyramid'3) Aging rate trend lines: Percentage of population aged 65+, grouped by country, with key inflection points annotated (e.g., Japan crossing into super-aged society in 2005, China projected to enter deep aging by 2035)4) Fertility rate vs. urbanization scatter plot: Analyze the correlation between the two, colored by development level, with anomalous countries labeled (e.g., Israel: high income yet high fertility)5) Demographic dividend window annotations: Define the dividend period as 'working-age population share > 65%', and mark each country's window opening and closing on the timeline6) Regional population growth contribution waterfall chart: Show each continent/region's share of global population growth from 2024 to 2050

name: Deep Research
prompts: Research the 10 best-performing AI models globally in 2026, conduct a comparative analysis of their key capabilities (including coding and multimodal performance), and summarize each model’s technical characteristics and best-fit use cases.
Analyze how business models in the AI Coding industry have evolved from 2023 to the present, identifying key inflection points and the underlying drivers.
Study the survival challenges and current conservation status of cheetah populations across the African savanna.
Study global gold price trends over the past decade, identify key drivers and the outlook ahead, and analyze how geopolitics, monetary policy, inflation, and other factors influence gold prices.

name: Product
prompts: I need to write a complete PRD for our B2B SaaS product. The feature is a “Team Collaboration Whiteboard”.Background:* Target users: product teams, design teams, remote teams* Problem: existing tools (Miro, FigJam) are too complex. Our users want a simpler collaboration experience integrated directly into our product.* We have notes from 5 user interviews. Users said:  * “Switching between tools is a hassle — I want to get everything done in one place.”  * “Miro has too many features. We only need basic sticky notes and connectors.”  * “Real-time collaboration matters, but we don’t need video/audio.”  * “I want to link directly to other objects in our product (tasks, docs).”  * “Mobile support isn’t important — we mainly use it on desktop.”Constraints:* Tech: must be compatible with our existing real-time sync architecture* Timeline: release MVP in Q2, full version in Q3* Team: 1 frontend engineer + 1 backend engineer, full-time for 3 months* Competitive positioning: simpler than Miro, more capable than Google JamboardSuccess metrics:* At least 30% of active teams use the whiteboard weekly* Average whiteboard session duration > 15 minutes (indicating deep usage)* Teams migrated from other collaboration tools (e.g., Miro) > 20%Please generate a complete PRD, including:* Problem statement and goals / non-goals* User stories (grouped by product teams, design teams, remote teams)* Requirements (MVP vs future)* Success metrics (leading and lagging indicators)* Technical considerations and dependencies* Open questions (engineering, design, legal, data)

I need to reprioritize the Q2 product roadmap. Here are the current five roadmap items:1. Mobile Offline Mode   * Description: Allow viewing and editing data without a network connection, then sync once back online   * Current status: Not started   * Estimated effort: 8 weeks (2 engineers)   * Request source: Mentioned by 20% of users in customer research2. Advanced Permission Management   * Description: Role-based access control (RBAC), with support for custom roles   * Current status: Not started   * Estimated effort: 6 weeks (2 engineers)   * Request source: Explicitly required by 5 enterprise customers; it is a deal blocker3. Data Visualization Dashboard   * Description: Built-in chart library so users can create custom dashboards   * Current status: Not started   * Estimated effort: 10 weeks (1 frontend + 1 backend)   * Request source: A strategic feature in the product vision, but not an urgent user need4. API Rate Limiting Optimization   * Description: Improve API performance to support higher concurrency   * Current status: In design   * Estimated effort: 4 weeks (1 backend engineer)   * Request source: Technical debt impacting 15% of large customers5. Slack Integration (Two-way Sync)   * Description: Create tasks from Slack, and push task updates back into Slack   * Current status: Not started   * Estimated effort: 5 weeks (1 backend engineer)   * Request source: Requested by 10 customers, including 2 high-intent prospects willing to payRecent changes:* Last week, Sales reported that 3 large customers (total ARR $180K) delayed signing because RBAC is missing* Our North Star metric is ARR growth, and enterprise customers are the primary growth driver* Engineering capacity: 10 weeks, 3 engineersPlease help me:1. Score each project using the RICE framework (Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort)2. Reorder priorities based on RICE scores and our current strategy (ARR growth)3. Produce the updated roadmap, indicating what ships in Q2 and what moves to Q34. Draft an update to the engineering team explaining why the priorities changed

We're planning a new feature: 'AI Auto-Generated Weekly Reports.' Help me create a comprehensive competitive analysis report.Analyze 5 competitors: Notion AI, Coda AI, ClickUp AI, Monday.com AI, Linear.Output:- Feature comparison matrix (dimensions: auto-generation capability, data source integration, custom templates, collaboration features, pricing)- User review summary (collect positive/negative feedback from G2, ProductHunt, etc.)- Differentiation strategy analysis for each product- Our opportunity areas: which needs are competitors not serving well?- Recommended differentiation direction (with rationale and validation suggestions)

Help me write quarterly product OKRs for Q3.Background:- We are a B2B collaboration tool- Q2 results: ARR $2M, 150 enterprise customers, NPS 42- Board mandate for Q3: double enterprise customer count, raise NPS to 50+Output:- 3 Objectives, each with 3-4 Key Results (following the SMART principle)- Responsible team and key initiatives for each KR- Risk factors and mitigation plans- Dependencies between OKRs

name: Slides Creation
prompts: Create a PowerPoint presentation on the development history of artificial intelligence (AI).
Create a PowerPoint presentation on 2026 trends in AI agents.
Create a minimalist-style PowerPoint presentation forCodeBuddy's 2025 year-end summary.
Create a PowerPoint presentation on POP MART's development history, with one IP character featured on every slide.

name: Design
prompts: Generate a page from this Figma component (Next.js App Router).Constraints:Reuse existing design tokens if provided (colors/type/spacing)No new dependencies unless necessaryHandle states: default / hover / disabled / loadingOutput: one page file + the related component file structure

Implement this Figma component as a production-ready UI section (React + TypeScript).Match layout/spacing/typography/radius/shadows/colors 1:1Split into semantic subcomponents (Container / Header / Content / Actions)Output: component code + required styles + a small usage example

Turn this Figma component into a runnable page (React).Fill with mock data (lists/titles/buttons as needed)Implement minimal interactions: click/selection/form validation (if present)Keep accessibility (ARIA + keyboard support)

Implement this Figma design as a responsive login page (React + Tailwind CSS). Requirements: pixel-perfect adaptation across three breakpoints (desktop/tablet/mobile), dark mode toggle, form validation (email format check, password strength indicator), social login buttons (Google/GitHub/WeChat), loading states, and error notification animations

name: Email editing
prompts: Draft a customer satisfaction survey email for [Product Name].
Write a churned-user win-back email for [Product Name].

Write a new feature launch announcement email for our SaaS product. The feature is 'AI Smart Assistant.' Requirements: eye-catching subject line, body highlighting 3 core selling points, GIF demo placeholder area, CTA guiding users to try it immediately, early-bird offer (free 30-day trial for the first 1,000 users).

Write a partner recruitment email — we're building a technology partner ecosystem for CodeBuddy. Requirements: describe partnership models (API integration / joint marketing / channel distribution), partner benefits (technical support, revenue sharing, co-branded exposure), success stories, application process, and contact information.

name: Video Generation
prompts: Create a 20-second vertical video (1080x1920, 30fps) showcasing the Global Top 10 AI Models leaderboard.Data source: OpenRouter.Requirements:- Each entry 1.5–2s with a bar chart animating from 0 to its value- Rank changes should be smooth (no jumping), numbers should have a counting animation- Clean background, consistent fonts, restrained color palette (max 2 primary colors)- 2s intro: title + data source in small text- 2s outro: one key insight summary
Generate an introduction video for OpenClaw

Create a 20-second 'Weekly AI News Roundup' video (1920x1080, 30fps).Content: 5 important AI news items from this week, fetched from real sources online.Requirements:- 2s intro: 'AI Weekly' title + this week's date range, title bounces in from top (spring effect)- Each news item 3s: headline slides in from the left, source and date in small text fades in from the bottom, a theme-related emoji or icon scales up on the right side- News items connected by a vertical timeline, timeline nodes light up as transition animation- 2s outro: 'Follow for more' CTA text with breathing animation- Dark solid background, white text, headlines use a bright accent color (e.g. #00D4FF) to highlight keywords- Overall pacing should be tight and information-dense

Create a 30-second programming language popularity decade-of-change video (1920x1080, 30fps).Data source: TIOBE or GitHub Octoverse, fetch real data online.Requirements:- Use a dynamic bar chart race to show Top 10 programming language ranking changes from 2015 to 2025- Each year stays for 2.5s, bars smoothly slide to their new positions (no jump cuts)- Bar colors match each language's official brand color (Python blue-yellow, JavaScript yellow, Rust orange, etc.)- Language name + real-time score with counting animation shown at the end of each bar- Year number in the top-right corner uses a flip transition effect- 2s intro: title 'Programming Language Trends' + data source in small text- 2s outro: freeze on the final ranking + one trend summary sentence (e.g. 'Python tops the chart for N consecutive years')- Dark gradient background (#0D1117 to #161B22), white text, overall style similar to GitHub dark theme

name: Daily Development
prompts: Help me add a new feature to the current project: [Feature description].
Help me refactor this code to improve readability and maintainability.
Help me fix this bug: [Bug description].
Help me optimize the performance of this feature. Current issue: [Performance issue description].
Help me add unit tests to this module, covering core logic.
Help me investigate this error: [Error message], find the cause and fix it.

name: Website Development
prompts: Help me develop a corporate website. Company name: [Company name], main business: [Business description]. Requirements: Include homepage, about us, products/services, and contact us pages with a clean, professional style.
Help me develop an admin dashboard for managing [Content to manage]. Requirements: Include login page, data dashboard, list management, and form editing with responsive layout.
Help me develop a personal blog website. Blog theme: [Blog theme]. Requirements: Include article list, article detail, tag categories, and about page with Markdown rendering support.
Help me develop an e-commerce homepage selling [Product type]. Requirements: Include carousel, product categories, featured products, and promotional sections.

name: Agent Apps
prompts: Use CodeBuddy SDK to create an Agent Web application for [Describe the Agent's purpose]. (Please use skill codebuddy-chat-web)
Use CodeBuddy SDK to initialize a chat application with functionality: [Describe chat functionality]. (Please use skill codebuddy-chat-web)

Use CodeBuddy SDK to create a client-side Agent Web application that implements [Describe project functionality]. (Please use skill codebuddy-chat-web, create a web app wrapped with Electron, ensuring it can be developed using a web browser)

Use CodeBuddy SDK to create an 'Intelligent Customer Service Agent' Web application. Requirements: multi-turn conversation support, FAQ knowledge base retrieval, automatic intent recognition (refund / order inquiry / technical support), auto-escalation to human agents when unresolved, persistent conversation history storage, and an admin dashboard to view chat logs and satisfaction statistics (Please use skill codebuddy-chat-web)

name: Skill Development
prompts: Help me create a Skill with functionality: [Describe Skill functionality]. Requirements: Define clear trigger words and execution logic.
Help me develop a file processing Skill that can [Describe file processing capabilities, e.g., batch rename, format conversion].
Help me develop a code generation Skill that auto-generates code based on [Describe input, e.g., database schema, API definitions].
Help me develop an automation Skill that implements [Describe automation task, e.g., scheduled backup, batch deployment].

name: CI/CD
prompts: Analyze the current project architecture and help me write a GitHub Actions workflow for automated build, test, and deployment.
Analyze the current project architecture and help me write a Dockerfile with optimized image size and multi-stage builds.
Analyze the current project architecture and help me write a Kubernetes Helm Chart including Deployment, Service, and ConfigMap.

name: Documentation
prompts: Analyze the current project's API endpoints and generate an OpenAPI v3 spec with complete descriptions and examples.
Analyze the current project and help me improve the README documentation with installation steps, usage examples, and contribution guidelines.
Analyze the current project architecture and help me create system architecture diagrams and design docs explaining core modules and data flow.

name: Web Design
prompts: Design a guitar brand website page, using two primary colors plus neutrals to build the entire page and strengthen brand recognition and contrast. Scene positioning: brand landing page, poster-style marketing, portfolio cover. Users expect a unified and distinctive two-color language. Visual design concept: pick one dark and one light or one warm and one cool primary color, and restrict core elements to this combination only; backgrounds can use gradients or large block divisions, with text kept in high contrast (use white/black strokes when necessary). Avoid introducing a third primary color that would break the purity. Material and texture: smooth gradients, simple geometry, low noise; buttons and cards echo the two-color scheme with solid fills or wireframes. Overall mood: unified, eye-catching, and strongly branded. The two-color palette delivers a clear tone, with clean visuals and memorable impact.
Design a sneaker culture introduction website page.

Design a creative design website page from a Neo-Brutalism designer's perspective, combining brutalism's hard edges with saturated candy color blocks, bold outlines, and offset shadows. Visual design concept: thick borders, large rounded corners or hard-angled blocks, saturated pink/yellow/blue/green, bold sans-serif or monospace fonts; prominent offset shadows. Use stickers/hand-drawn elements to add playfulness while keeping layout divisions clear. Material and texture: solid fills, noise/stripe backgrounds, sticker/graffiti symbols; shadows are short but with clear offset, borders 2-4px. Avoid excessive glass and gradients. Overall mood: bold, playful yet orderly, like an interface made of colorful building blocks.

name: Mobile App
prompts: Design a fitness App. Create a complete user registration flow and a workout data dashboard page for a fitness tracking App, with charts that display daily exercise data.
Referencing Xiaohongshu's (Little Red Book) design style, help me design a discovery page for a content community App, featuring a waterfall card layout and search/filter functionality.
Referencing Instagram's style, design a login page mockup for a social App.

name: Design System
prompts: Help me build a complete Design System, including a color system, typography hierarchy, spacing rules, and foundational component specification documentation.
Referencing Ant Design's style, design a component library specification for my admin management system, including button, form, table, and modal components.
Create a Design Token system for a mobile App that supports light/dark theme switching, and output a complete variable table for colors, border radii, and shadows.

name: Web App
prompts: Design a complete interface for a project management WebApp, including a dashboard overview, task kanban, team member management, and project settings pages. Style inspired by Linear—clean and efficient.

Design an online collaborative whiteboard WebApp, including a canvas editing area, toolbar, layers panel, and multi-user collaboration status indicators. Supports light and dark themes with a modern minimalist style.

Design a SaaS data analytics platform WebApp interface, including data source connections, a visualization chart editor, report generation, and permission management modules. Professional business color scheme.

name: PPT Design
prompts: Help me generate a set of launch event slide mockups for a Chinese-style wuxia open-world exploration game, including multiple main pages. The layout should be large and suitable for big-screen playback at the venue. Boldly use SVG decorations on the slides, keep page elements rich and refined, and showcase the game's art design, UI design, rich content, and other elements. Use "ai" mode wherever images need to be generated.
Create a 6-page PPT introducing Minecraft, with a cool and rich pixel-style layout and a pixel-themed design style.
Help me design a presentation deck for internal training on the topic of new employee onboarding, covering company culture, workflows, and commonly used tools.

name: Interactive Prototype
prompts: Help me design an interactive prototype for a task management tool, including switching between board view and list view, as well as task drag-and-drop reordering.
Design an interactive prototype for an online collaborative document, focusing on multi-user real-time editing cursors, comments/annotations, and version history rollback.
Design an interactive prototype for a data visualization large-screen display, supporting time-range filtering, chart drill-down, and full-screen display mode switching.

name: Brand Design
prompts: Design a tech-style brand Logo with deep blue as the primary color, suitable for an AI product. It should be simple and recognizable, and also provide standard color values and usage guidelines.
Design a complete visual identity system for my coffee brand, including Logo, color palette, typography guidelines, and business card style, with a warm and artistic style.
Referencing the design styles of Apple and Stripe, design the brand identity for my SaaS product, including the main Logo, icon variations, and brand color palette.

name: Icons & Illustrations
prompts: Help me design a set of 24 linear-style general-purpose icons suitable for a backend management system, covering common scenarios such as Home, Users, Settings, and Data.
Design a set of cute-style illustrated icons for a children's education App, covering 12 scenes across the themes of reading, math, music, and science.
Design a set of flat-style financial icons covering 16 scenarios such as payment, billing, wallet, transfer, and wealth management, with a unified color scheme.

name: Visual Posters
prompts: Help me design a promotional poster for a product launch event with the theme of AI innovation, size 1080x1920, highlighting the launch date and product highlights.
Design a set of e-commerce Banners for the Double 11 sales event, 3 banners in different sizes with a unified style, emphasizing discount information and a limited-time flash sale atmosphere.
Design an event poster for a tech summit, highlighting the headshots of three speakers, the event time and location, and the registration QR code.
